title: 10 Recipes Using a Cookie Dough Scoop for Mini Muffins
meta_description: Discover 10 delicious mini muffin recipes you can whip up using a cookie dough scoop. From sweet to savory, learn how the Cookie Dough Scoop guarantees perfect,
html:
When you reach for a cookie dough scoop mini muffins recipe, the first thing you’ll notice is how the Cookie Dough Scoop makes portion control effortless. The spring‑release handles let you drop the exact amount of batter into each liner, so every muffin is the same size, bakes evenly, and looks professional. Below are ten versatile mini‑muffin ideas that take full advantage of that uniformity—perfect for breakfast, snack time, or a party platter.

1. Classic Blueberry Mini Muffins

Nothing beats a simple blueberry muffin, and the mini version is just as satisfying. Using a 1‑inch scoop, fill each paper liner about three‑quarters full. The batter spreads just enough to create a golden top without over‑mixing.

  • 1 cup all‑purpose flour
  • ½ cup sugar
  • 2 tsp baking powder
  • ¼ tsp salt
  • ½ cup milk
  • ¼ cup melted butter
  • 1 large egg
  • 1 tsp vanilla extract
  • 1 cup fresh blueberries

Combine dry ingredients, whisk wet ingredients separately, then fold together gently before adding the berries. Bake at 375°F for 12‑14 minutes.

2. Chocolate‑Chip Banana Mini Muffins

Banana adds moisture, while chocolate chips give a sweet surprise in every bite. The Cookie Dough Scoop ensures each muffin gets the same banana‑chip ratio, so none are over‑ or under‑filled.

Mix ½ cup mashed ripe banana with ¼ cup melted coconut oil, ¼ cup honey, and one egg. Stir in ¾ cup flour, ¼ tsp baking soda, a pinch of salt, and ¼ cup chocolate chips. Scoop and bake at 350°F for 10‑12 minutes.

3. Savory Cheddar & Bacon Mini Muffins

Turn the mini muffin tin into a bite‑size brunch staple. The uniform scoops keep the bacon pieces evenly distributed, preventing any one muffin from becoming too greasy.

Ingredients include 1 cup flour, ½ tsp baking powder, ¼ tsp salt, ¼ cup shredded sharp cheddar, ¼ cup cooked bacon crumbles, ¼ cup milk, and 1 egg. After mixing, scoop and bake at 375°F for 13‑15 minutes. Serve warm.

4. Lemon Poppy‑Seed Mini Muffins

Bright citrus flavor and a pop of texture make these mini muffins a crowd‑pleaser. Using the scoop, you’ll get consistent crumb structure, which is crucial for the delicate lemon glaze that tops them.

Whisk together 1 cup flour, ½ tsp baking powder, ¼ tsp salt, ¼ cup sugar, 2 tbsp poppy seeds, zest of one lemon, ¼ cup lemon juice, ¼ cup milk, ¼ cup melted butter, and one egg. Scoop, bake at 350°F for 11‑13 minutes, then drizzle with a simple lemon glaze (powdered sugar + lemon juice).

5. Apple Cinnamon Oat Mini Muffins

For a healthier option, combine oats, diced apples, and warm cinnamon. The scoop’s spring‑release design lets you press the batter gently into the liners, creating a slightly denser texture that holds up well with the fruit.

Blend ¾ cup rolled oats, ¾ cup whole‑wheat flour, 1 tsp baking powder, ½ tsp cinnamon, ¼ tsp salt, ¼ cup brown sugar, ½ cup diced apples, ¼ cup milk, ¼ cup applesauce, and one egg. Scoop and bake at 350°F for 12‑14 minutes.

6. Peanut Butter & Jelly Mini Muffins

Kids love the PB&J combo, and these mini muffins deliver that nostalgic flavor without the extra carbs of a sandwich. The Cookie Dough Scoop makes it easy to alternate peanut‑butter‑filled and jelly‑topped muffins for a visually appealing tray.

Prepare a basic vanilla batter (1 cup flour, ½ tsp baking powder, ¼ tsp salt, ¼ cup sugar, ¼ cup milk, ¼ cup peanut butter, 1 egg). Scoop half the batter, add a dollop of jelly, then top with the remaining batter. Bake at 375°F for 10‑12 minutes.

7. Mini Cornbread Muffins with Jalapeño

Spice up your taco night with mini cornbread muffins. The uniform scoops help keep the jalapeño pieces evenly spread, so each bite has the right amount of heat.

Mix ¾ cup cornmeal, ¼ cup flour, 1 tsp baking powder, ¼ tsp salt, 1 egg, ½ cup buttermilk, ¼ cup melted butter, ¼ cup shredded cheddar, and 1 finely chopped jalapeño. Scoop and bake at 400°F for 9‑11 minutes.

8. Coconut Lime Mini Muffins

These tropical treats are a hit at summer BBQs. The Cookie Dough Scoop guarantees each muffin has the same coconut‑lime balance, preventing any overly sweet or sour spots.

Combine 1 cup flour, ½ tsp baking powder, ¼ tsp salt, ¼ cup shredded coconut, zest of one lime, ¼ cup lime juice, ¼ cup coconut milk, ¼ cup sugar, and one egg. Scoop and bake at 350°F for 12‑13 minutes.

9. Vegan Pumpkin Spice Mini Muffins

Perfect for autumn, these vegan muffins use pumpkin puree and flax “egg” to bind the batter. The scoop’s consistent size helps the muffins rise uniformly, giving a tidy dome on each liner.

Blend ¾ cup whole‑wheat flour, ¼ cup oat flour, 1 tsp pumpkin pie spice, ½ tsp baking soda, ¼ tsp salt, ½ cup pumpkin puree, ¼ cup maple syrup, ¼ cup almond milk, 1 tbsp ground flaxseed mixed with 3 tbsp water, and ¼ cup oil. Scoop and bake at 350°F for 13‑15 minutes.

10. Mini Muffin “Meatball” Bites

Yes, you can use the same scoop for savory meatball‑style muffins. Mix ground turkey or beef with breadcrumbs, herbs, and a touch of cheese. The spring‑release feature lets you drop each meatball into a muffin cup, creating a portable protein snack.

Combine ½ lb ground meat, ¼ cup breadcrumbs, ¼ cup grated Parmesan, 1 tbsp chopped parsley, 1 egg, salt, and pepper. Scoop into greased mini muffin tins and bake at 375°F for 12‑14 minutes. Serve with a drizzle of marinara.

FAQ

Can I use a cookie dough scoop for batter that’s thicker than cookie dough?

Yes. The spring‑release mechanism works with most batters, from thick cookie dough to slightly runny pancake batter. If the batter is extremely liquid, you may want a larger scoop or a spoon.

Do I need to grease the mini‑muffin tin when using a cookie dough scoop?

While the scoop helps release batter cleanly, greasing the tin (or using paper liners) ensures the muffins slide out easily, especially for recipes with higher sugar or butter content.

How do I clean the Cookie Dough Scoop after making savory recipes?

Rinse the scoop immediately after use to prevent food from drying on the metal. For stubborn residue, soak in warm, soapy water for a few minutes, then scrub with a soft brush. The stainless‑steel body is dishwasher‑safe, but hand‑washing preserves the spring‑release feel.
